title: toxicodendron radicans subsp. negundo
intro: Toxicodendron radicans subsp. negundo, also known as the poison ivy vine, is a subspecies of the poisonous plant Toxicodendron radicans. While its sap is highly allergenic and can cause skin irritation, it is also valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Toxicodendron radicans subsp. negundo can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, but caution should be exercised when handling the plant due to its potent allergens.
